summary: Backend-only testing of pentest security fixes: IP-based login rate limiting (HTTP 429 after 10 req/15min), per-account lockout (HTTP 423 after 5 failed attempts/15min), Retry-After headers, and lockout counter clear on successful login. All 5 tests passed (100%). Frontend pagination and scroll-to-top were NOT tested per request (backend-only).

minor.issue: Inconsistent 423 behavior on 5th failed attempt: when user email is unknown, the 5th attempt returns 401 (no lockout exception in user-not-found branch) even though account_lockout.record_failed_attempt() returns locked=True. Lockout only applies from the 6th call onward via is_locked() guard. For wrong-password path (existing user), the 5th attempt returns 423 as expected. Behavior is still secure (lockout is enforced), but the 5th response is semantically inconsistent between the two failure paths.

IP rate limiter uses first X-Forwarded-For value, which in multi-hop proxy/egress setups may rotate. In this test environment two egress IPs (34.16.56.64, 35.225.230.28) were observed, so a legitimate attacker behind a NAT pool can effectively double their quota. Consider rate-limiting on /24 subnet or combining IP+User-Agent fingerprint for stricter enforcement.

action_items: OPTIONAL: For consistency, have the user-not-found branch in /api/auth/login also raise 423 when record_failed_attempt() returns True (same as wrong-password path) so the 5th failed attempt returns 423 regardless of account existence.
OPTIONAL: Consider broader IP scoping (e.g., /24 block) or IP+UA fingerprint for the IP rate limiter to reduce evasion via egress-IP rotation.
critical_code_review_comments: middleware/security.py IPLoginRateLimiter: correct implementation with 10 req/15 min window; record_attempt sets block when len >= 10 so the 11th request returns 429.
middleware/security.py AccountLockoutManager: correct 5 attempts/15 min; locks account and clears in-memory counter on lock_account; clear_attempts() on successful login.
server.py login(): IP check runs before account check - correct precedence. Retry-After headers set on both 429 (900s) and 423 (dynamic seconds_remaining or 900s).
server.py login(): account_lockout.clear_attempts(email) called after successful password verification - confirmed working via test.
In-memory state means rate-limit counters reset on backend restart; acceptable for single-worker deployment but would break horizontal scaling (documented limitation).
seed_data_creation: None - used existing admin account; test emails are ephemeral fake emails with UUID suffixes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend has single uvicorn worker so in-memory IPLoginRateLimiter/AccountLockoutManager state persists during test runs. Tests use `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` via session/function fixtures to reset state. Test environment egress rotates between 2 IPs (34.16.56.64, 35.225.230.28) so IP-rate-limit tests send extra requests (25-30) to guarantee at least one egress IP exceeds the 10-request threshold. Frontend features (pagination, scroll-to-top) were NOT tested this iteration - main agent confirmed via screenshots.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no bugs to RCA. Two initial test failures were due to (a) class-scoped fixture causing in-memory state to leak between tests (fixed by adding function-scoped fixture for counter-clear test) and (b) test egress IP load-balancing across two IPs (fixed by raising request count to guarantee threshold crossing on one egress IP).
